title: Glandora diffusa
intro: Glandora diffusa, also known as Rock Mint or Glandular Rockrose, is a flowering perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Glandora diffusa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Glandora diffusa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried flowers and leaves, which can be used in potpourri and as a natural insect repellent.
taxonomy: Glandora diffusa is classified in the family Boraginaceae. It is a species of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is also known as the spreading gilia or diffuse gilia. Glandora diffusa is a member of the genus Glandora, which is part of the Boraginaceae family. This classification makes it a part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms.
